description: Fix four concrete bugs in the Chrome extension: (1) handlePluginReadinessChanged leaks an open message channel on every readiness event by never calling sendResponse; (2) resolvePerTabSettings fails silently for url-type settings on plugins whose instanceMap patterns use https:// or http:// scheme instead of *://, returning empty string; (3) BrowserToolsCard.handleToolPermissionChange stores pre-toggle state in a single ref that is overwritten on concurrent calls, causing incorrect rollback when multiple tool permission changes fail simultaneously; (4) network-capture.ts onDetach handler omits cleanupThrottle, leaving stale throttle state when DevTools are opened, breaking subsequent throttle tool calls.

userStories.title: Fix handlePluginReadinessChanged to call sendResponse
userStories.description: The handlePluginReadinessChanged message handler never calls sendResponse, but the background message listener unconditionally returns true for all registered handlers, leaving an orphaned Chrome message channel open on every plugin:readinessChanged event. Fix it by calling sendResponse({ ok: true }) immediately (fire-and-forget pattern, matching handleToolProgress).
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginReadinessChanged calls sendResponse({ ok: true }) before or after starting the async IIFE
The _sendResponse parameter is renamed to sendResponse (underscore removed)
No other handler logic is changed

userStories.title: Fix resolvePerTabSettings host extraction to handle https:// and http:// patterns
userStories.description: resolvePerTabSettings in iife-injection.ts extracts the host from instanceMap patterns using `pattern.replace('*://', '').replace('/*', '')`. This only works for *:// patterns. For https://host/* or http://host/* patterns, the replace has no match and leaves the scheme prefix in the extracted string, causing the host comparison to always fail and url-type settings to silently resolve to empty string.
acceptanceCriteria: resolvePerTabSettings correctly extracts the hostname for *://host/*, https://host/*, and http://host/* patterns
For a tabUrl of https://app.example.com/path and instanceMap { work: 'https://app.example.com/*' }, matchedInstance resolves to 'work'
For a tabUrl of https://app.example.com/path and instanceMap { work: '*://app.example.com/*' }, matchedInstance still resolves to 'work' (unchanged behavior)
When no instance matches, the fallback behavior (logging a warning and setting resolved[key] = '') is unchanged

userStories.title: Fix BrowserToolsCard rollback race condition with per-invocation snapshot
userStories.description: BrowserToolsCard.handleToolPermissionChange stores the pre-toggle tool list in a single preToggleRef that is overwritten on every invocation. When multiple tool permission changes are made concurrently (e.g., user quickly toggles two tools) and both server requests fail, the myVersion guard skips all but the last rollback, leaving earlier tools stuck in incorrect permission states. Fix by storing per-invocation snapshots in a Map keyed by version number, matching the pattern used by PluginCard.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria: handleToolPermissionChange stores the pre-toggle state per myVersion in a Map (not a single ref)
On failure, each invocation rolls back exactly its own tool to its own pre-toggle permission regardless of other concurrent calls
The preToggleRef is removed from the component
The rollback Map entries are deleted in the finally/catch cleanup, not left to accumulate

userStories.title: Add missing cleanupThrottle call to debugger.onDetach handler
userStories.description: The chrome.debugger.onDetach handler in network-capture.ts calls cleanupInterception and cleanupEmulation but not cleanupThrottle. When DevTools are opened (externally detaching the debugger) while throttling is active, throttlingTabs retains the stale entry. The next browser_throttle_network call skips re-attaching the debugger because isThrottling returns true, then chrome.debugger.sendCommand fails with 'Debugger is not attached' until the extension reloads.
acceptanceCriteria: chrome.debugger.onDetach handler calls cleanupThrottle(tabId) alongside cleanupInterception(tabId) and cleanupEmulation(tabId)
cleanupThrottle is imported from ./browser-commands/throttle-commands.js in network-capture.ts (if not already imported)
